summary: Retest of iteration_1 HIGH bug (Mongo id serialization). Backend now emits `id` instead of `_id` on /api/contact and /api/blog (list, get-by-slug, POST, PUT) thanks to response_model_by_alias=False. All 13 pytest cases pass. Frontend admin CRUD flows (mark-read, delete-contact, create/edit/delete post) all succeed end-to-end via UI — testids on rows now contain real 24-char ObjectIds. Public /blog list and article pages still render fine.

minor.issue: Requires full BlogCreate body (title + content min 5); not a partial update. Not a bug, but forces frontend to send full form on any edit (which it does).

issues: When calling `page.fill` on all inputs but not touching the shadcn Select service field, the submit didn't seem to persist a record (created via UI in this run). Not reproducible cleanly — form validation may silently reject. Consider surfacing form errors after submit for the debug flow.

frontend_playwright: Messages tab: every contact row testid contains a real 24-char ObjectId (no `undefined`)
Admin PATCH /api/contact/{id}/read via mark-read button -> button removed after read
Admin DELETE /api/contact/{id} via del-contact button -> row removed
Blog tab: every post row testid contains real ObjectId
Create blog post (POST /api/blog) returns 201 and row appears with real id in testid
Edit blog post (PUT /api/blog/{id}) via edit-post -> title update persists
Delete blog post (DELETE /api/blog/{id}) via del-post -> row removed
Public /blog renders cards; article page renders correctly with title/content

critical_code_review_comments: server.py list_contacts / list_blog build models with dict spread `{**d, '_id': str(d['_id'])}` in every response — fine but a tiny helper `serialize_doc(d)` would DRY it up.
PUT /api/blog/{post_id} rebuilds full body (BlogCreate) — acceptable, but an optional Patch route would enable partial edits.
server.py declares two @app.on_event handlers (startup/shutdown) — deprecated in FastAPI 0.109+. Consider migrating to lifespan context manager.
server.py `mongo_url = os.environ['MONGO_URL']` and `db = client[os.environ['DB_NAME']]` will KeyError at import time if env missing — acceptable fail-fast but hides root cause during tests.
test_credentials: Admin: rewllasy@gmail.com / MoukisTech2025! (Bearer JWT in localStorage 'moukis_token')
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up test contacts (TEST_UI_MarkDelete) and blog posts (TEST_UI_Article_E2E, TEST_UI_Article_E2E_EDITED). Pytest also creates/deletes TEST_Alice, TEST_Bob contacts and TEST_Post_* posts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend `_id`/`id` serialization bug from iteration_1 is fully fixed. Both /api/contact and /api/blog use response_model_by_alias=False, so JSON emits `id`. Admin CRUD (mark-read, del-contact, create/edit/del post) work end-to-end. Regression pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (13 tests, all pass). Public contact form UI submit is a bit finicky (may silently skip when Select service left untouched) — seed contacts via API for reliability.
rca of the issue: Iteration_1 issue: BlogPost & Contact Pydantic models used Field(alias='_id'). When FastAPI serialized via response_model (default), it wrote the alias '_id' to JSON. Frontend AdminDashboard reads `p.id` and `c.id` -> undefined -> DELETE /api/blog/undefined -> 500. Fix applied by main agent: add response_model_by_alias=False to all list/get/POST/PUT routes returning these models. Verified via curl and pytest that responses now contain `id` (24-char string) and no `_id`. UI now correctly targets rows by real ids and all destructive/mutation ops succeed.
